title: primula tschuktschorum var. pumila
intro: Primula tschuktschorum var. pumila, also known as dwarf Tschuktschorum primrose, is a rare and compact variety of alpine primrose native to the Arctic tundra. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Primula tschuktschorum var. pumila include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
